notes: Maps to KeyRack 'Creating' state. Covers async HSM provisioning before key is usable
Maps to KeyRack 'Enabled' state. Full encrypt + decrypt + sign + verify
Maps to KeyRack 'Disabled' state. Decrypt/verify only — no new encrypt/sign
No distinct 'Compromised' state. Disabled blocks encrypt but still allows decrypt. A compromised key should block ALL operations
Maps to KeyRack 'Destroyed' state. Material erased via provider. Cascade-disable for hierarchy
No distinct state. Would combine Compromised + Destroyed. Relevant for audit trail completeness
OsRng (CSPRNG) for software provider. HSM RNG for PKCS#11. Per-algorithm generation via CryptoProvider
AES-256 (256-bit), Ed25519 (128-bit), P-256 (128-bit), RSA-3072 (128-bit). RSA-2048 is 112-bit — below 128-bit recommendation
Keys bound to provider; data keys encrypted under CMK. gRPC/REST over TLS. No plaintext key transport
PKCS#11: HSM non-volatile storage. Software: in-memory with zeroization. Metadata: postgres/sqlite (no key material)
Configurable rotation policies (90-2560 days). Versioned rotation creates new key material
KeyUsage enum (EncryptDecrypt, SignVerify) enforced per key. Cannot sign with an encryption key or vice versa
Cooperative rotation protocol. New version generated; old version transitions to decrypt-only. Policy-driven auto-rotation
Rotation policies define intervals. No minimum cryptoperiod enforcement per algorithm type. No per-key operation counter for nonce budget
'Disabled' state partially covers this (decrypt/verify only). No distinct 'Archived' state for long-term verification-only retention
Software: zeroize crate (write_volatile). PKCS#11: HSM destroy command. Scheduled deletion with grace period
Destruction event emitted to audit trail. No signed destruction certificate. HSM destruction depends on vendor verification mechanisms
AES-256-GCM. 256-bit security strength. FIPS 197 + SP 800-38D
FIPS 186-5 approved. 128-bit security strength. Pure mode (RFC 8032)
FIPS 186-4 approved. 128-bit security strength
PKCS#1v1.5 only — PSS not yet supported. RSA-2048 (112-bit), RSA-3072 (128-bit), RSA-4096 (152-bit)
RSA-2048 provides 112-bit security — below the 128-bit minimum recommendation. Should emit deprecation warning on creation. Deadline: 2030
No PQC algorithms (ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA) implemented. Architecture supports extension via CryptoProvider trait. Roadmap: V2
CryptoProvider trait + KeySpec enum allow algorithm addition without architecture changes. Ciphertext header versioning supports format migration
